DISCOVERY 612, PRESENT: JERRY N. GANS, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFFS/MOVING PARTIES DAVID PAFFORD AND TINA PAFFORD. DAVID J. GARTHE, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY. HEARING HELD. RULING OF COURT AFTER HEARING. TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATION: (TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES OF FORMER DEL MONTE EMPLOYEE GARY PLOTNER AT DEPOSITION AND FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER RE: FUTURE INSTRUCTIONS NOT TO RESPOND TO DEPOSITION QUESTIONS NOT INVOLVING PRIVILEGE IS GRANTED AS IT SEEKS FURTHER DEPOSITION TO OBTAIN RESPONSES TO THE QUESTIONS POSED. BY THIS RULING THE COURT DOES NOT DETERMINE THAT THE QUESTIONS ARE PROPER, BUT THE PROPER PROCEDURE WOULD BE TO OBJECT UPON THE GROUNDS OFFERED IN THE OPPOSITION, INCLUDING LACK OF FOUNDATION, AND THEN ALLOWING THE WITNESS TO ANSWER AS HE IS ABLE. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED). MODIFICATION: DEFENDANT SHALL FILE A MODIFIED DECLARATION RE. FOUNDATION FOR PLOTNER'S STATUS AS A CONSULTANT, THEREBY JUSTIFYING AN OBJECTION THAT TESTIMONY IS PRIVILEGED WORK PRODUCT. DEPOSITION TO BE RECONVENED. QUESTIONS MAY BE POSED REGARDING STATEMENTS TO CAL OSHA AND THE BASIS FOR THOSE STATEMENTS. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
